lessons.title: Stage 1 · Core Vowels
lessons.subtitle: Start decoding the shape of Korean sounds
lessons.learningObjective: Recognize the core vertical and horizontal vowels and hear the difference between them.

vocabulary.memoryHook: A line with one right arm saying ah.
The arm points left, so it is not plain ah.
The short line reaches upward like saying oh.
The short line hangs below like a bowl saying oo.

pronunciationTips.description: These are often confused by beginners because both are rounded and short.
pronunciationTips.englishComparison: ㅓ is closer to 'uh', while ㅗ is a cleaner 'oh'.
pronunciationTips.commonMistake: Do not rely only on romanization. Watch the line direction and listen repeatedly.

memoryHooks.story: Vertical vowels reach sideways, horizontal vowels reach up or down. The shape tells you how the sound moves.
memoryHooks.visualDescription: One family grows arms left and right, the other grows stems above and below.

scriptItems.text: Hangul becomes much easier when you learn the shapes as sound families.
scriptItems.romanization: hangul becomes much easier when you learn the shapes as sound families
scriptItems.translation: Learn the pattern, not just the label.

speech.speechText: 한글은 소리 모양으로 배우면 훨씬 쉬워져요.

scriptItems.text: ㅏ ㅓ are vertical vowels. ㅗ ㅜ are horizontal vowels.
scriptItems.romanization: a eo are vertical vowels, o u are horizontal vowels

speech.speechText: 아 어는 세로 모음이고 오 우는 가로 모음이에요.

writingTargets.prompt: Trace ㅓ and hear the left arm.

dialogueItems.title: Mini listening
dialogueItems.comprehensionQuestion: Which one has the short line above?

checkpointItems.prompt: Which symbol is the vertical vowel with one right arm?
checkpointItems.explanation: ㅏ is the core vertical vowel with one right arm.

lessons.title: Stage 2 · Core Consonants
lessons.subtitle: Meet the shapes that anchor each syllable
lessons.learningObjective: Recognize the most common beginner consonants and connect their shape to mouth movement.

vocabulary.memoryHook: A corner that starts many first syllables.
A bend you can lean on while saying nnn.
A closed square like lips for mmm.

pronunciationTips.description: The sound shifts between soft g and final k depending on position.
pronunciationTips.englishComparison: Softer than a heavily aspirated English k.
pronunciationTips.commonMistake: Do not blast too much air.

memoryHooks.story: Many Hangul consonants feel like little blueprints for what your mouth is doing.
memoryHooks.visualDescription: Corner, box, circle, and peaks all hint at sound shape.

scriptItems.text: A Hangul block usually starts with a consonant and then joins a vowel.
scriptItems.romanization: a hangul block usually starts with a consonant and then joins a vowel
scriptItems.translation: Think building blocks, not isolated letters.

speech.speechText: 한글 글자는 보통 자음으로 시작해서 모음과 붙어요.

dialogueItems.title: Name the letter
dialogueItems.comprehensionQuestion: What was the answer letter name?

checkpointItems.prompt: Which consonant looks like a closed square mouth?
checkpointItems.explanation: ㅁ is often remembered as a mouth closed into a box.

lessons.title: Stage 3 · Build Syllables
lessons.subtitle: Combine letters into readable Hangul blocks
lessons.learningObjective: Read beginner CV syllables by combining consonants and vowels.

pronunciationTips.description: Read the consonant first, then let the vowel finish the block.
pronunciationTips.englishComparison: Think of them as one beat, not two separate letters.
pronunciationTips.commonMistake: Do not pause between the consonant and vowel.

memoryHooks.story: Korean is not a long string of letters. It is a set of sound blocks snapped together.
memoryHooks.visualDescription: Tiny Lego blocks that lock into one readable unit.

scriptItems.text: Now we stop naming letters and start reading blocks.
scriptItems.romanization: now we stop naming letters and start reading blocks
scriptItems.translation: This is where Hangul starts feeling useful.

speech.speechText: 이제는 글자 이름보다 글자 블록을 읽기 시작해요.

lessons.title: Stage 4 · Tense And Aspirated Sounds
lessons.subtitle: Hear the difference in strength and airflow
lessons.learningObjective: Distinguish tense consonants and aspirated consonants from the soft base sounds.

vocabulary.memoryHook: Two ㄱ shapes pressed tightly together.
Like ㄱ but with extra breath.

pronunciationTips.description: These are not the same sound family. One is soft, one is tense, one is aspirated.
pronunciationTips.englishComparison: Tense means tighter voice onset; aspirated means more air.
pronunciationTips.commonMistake: Do not flatten all three into one English k.

memoryHooks.story: Tense sounds squeeze. Aspirated sounds puff. The shape family helps you remember which one you hear.
memoryHooks.visualDescription: Pressed shapes versus shapes with extra air marks.

scriptItems.text: Korean often distinguishes softness, tension, and breath within one consonant family.
scriptItems.romanization: korean often distinguishes softness tension and breath within one consonant family
scriptItems.translation: This is why careful listening matters.

speech.speechText: 한국어는 같은 자음 가족 안에서도 세기와 숨소리가 달라요.

writingTargets.prompt: Trace ㄲ and feel the tension.

dialogueItems.title: Hear the contrast

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: I can hear three different starts.
lines.translation: You are training your ear for contrast.

speech.speechText: 세 가지 시작 소리를 들을 수 있어요.

dialogueItems.comprehensionQuestion: Which sound had the strongest puff of air?

checkpointItems.prompt: Which option is the tense consonant?
checkpointItems.explanation: ㄲ is the tense double consonant.

lessons.title: Stage 5 · Compound Vowels
lessons.subtitle: Read blended vowels without freezing
lessons.learningObjective: Decode blended vowel shapes like ㅐ, ㅔ, ㅘ, and ㅝ without relying on romanization.

vocabulary.memoryHook: One extra arm grows out of ㅏ to make ㅐ.

pronunciationTips.description: Modern Korean often pronounces these very similarly, but you should still learn both spellings.
pronunciationTips.englishComparison: Focus on recognition even when the sound is close.
pronunciationTips.commonMistake: Do not assume one symbol covers every e-like sound.

memoryHooks.story: A compound vowel is a tiny shape recipe. Once you know the parts, the whole block stops looking scary.
memoryHooks.visualDescription: One vowel shape snapping into another like puzzle pieces.

scriptItems.text: Compound vowels look busy, but most of them are just familiar pieces combined.
scriptItems.romanization: compound vowels look busy but most of them are just familiar pieces combined
scriptItems.translation: Decode the ingredients, then read the result.

speech.speechText: 복합 모음은 복잡해 보여도 이미 배운 조각이 합쳐진 경우가 많아요.

writingTargets.prompt: Trace ㅐ and notice the extra arm.

dialogueItems.title: Blend the shape
dialogueItems.comprehensionQuestion: What blended vowel did you make?

checkpointItems.prompt: Which option is built from ㅗ + ㅏ ?
checkpointItems.explanation: ㅘ is the blended wa vowel.

lessons.title: Stage 6 · Batchim Basics
lessons.subtitle: Notice the final consonant at the bottom
lessons.learningObjective: Spot common final consonants and read short useful words with more confidence.

vocabulary.memoryHook: The ㅂ sound closes at the bottom as a final p-like sound.
ㄹ sits at the bottom and closes the block.

pronunciationTips.description: A final consonant changes how the syllable ends and often sounds shorter than English learners expect.
pronunciationTips.englishComparison: You often hear the syllable cut off more quickly.
pronunciationTips.commonMistake: Do not add an extra vowel after the final consonant.

memoryHooks.story: Batchim means support. The final consonant sits under the block like a support beam holding the syllable up.
memoryHooks.visualDescription: A floor beam under a syllable block.

scriptItems.text: Once you see the final consonant at the bottom, many beginner words suddenly become readable.
scriptItems.romanization: once you see the final consonant at the bottom many beginner words suddenly become readable
scriptItems.translation: This is a big confidence jump for new learners.

speech.speechText: 받침이 보이기 시작하면 초급 단어가 훨씬 잘 읽혀요.

writingTargets.prompt: Review a simple open block before closing sounds.

dialogueItems.title: A real beginner word

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: I can hear the final ㄹ in 물.
lines.translation: You are noticing the bottom consonant.

dialogueItems.comprehensionQuestion: Which word in the dialogue had a final ㄹ ?

checkpointItems.prompt: Which word below has a final consonant at the bottom?
checkpointItems.explanation: 밥 ends with a final ㅂ batchim.
